columns.extraction_plan: Liver metastases are not reported separately for the treatment arm. The PDF reports "Visceral metastases, n (%)", as 28 (15%) for the ADT plus D arm in Table 1 on page 3, but this category combines liver and lung metastases and does not provide individual counts for liver.

Liver metastases are not reported separately for the control arm. The PDF reports "Visceral metastases, n (%)", as 23 (12%) for the ADT arm in Table 1 on page 3, but this category combines liver and lung metastases and does not provide individual counts for liver.

Lung metastases are not reported separately for the treatment arm. The PDF reports "Visceral metastases, n (%)", as 28 (15%) for the ADT plus D arm in Table 1 on page 3, but this category combines liver and lung metastases and does not provide individual counts for lungs.

Lung metastases are not reported separately for the control arm. The PDF reports "Visceral metastases, n (%)", as 23 (12%) for the ADT arm in Table 1 on page 3, but this category combines liver and lung metastases and does not provide individual counts for lungs.
In Table 1 on page 3, under the "ADT plus D (n = 192)", column, locate the row "Bone metastases, n (%)". The value is 155 (81%).
In Table 1 on page 3, under the "ADT (n = 193)", column, locate the row "Bone metastases, n (%)". The value is 156 (81%).
Nodal metastases are not reported separately for the treatment arm. The PDF does not provide specific data for nodal metastases.
Nodal metastases are not reported separately for the control arm. The PDF does not provide specific data for nodal metastases.
